reasoning: C had the most genuinely distinct set: it explicitly broke a different governing assumption per version and the assumptions held under scrutiny, with the Silent Hour Exchange (coordinated quiet as cross-firm infrastructure that only works at scale) and the Omertà Underwriter (secrecy reinsured and priced in basis points like catastrophe risk) being the freshest non-adjacent frames, plus Negative Music as authored, copyrighted, royalty-metered silence. B is the most vivid prose with its named human needs, but it clusters: the Negative Easement and the Quiet Index are both silence-as-tradable-property, costing diversity. A has real spread across five concepts (Quiet Credits, Absence Insurance, Silence Exchange) but is the most compressed and least developed of the three, and shares the easement/credits and confession-vault ideas with C without pushing them as far.
